Aussie Olympians have hilariously reviewed their first night of rest in the village beds that were constructed for the Tokyo games in 2021 amid a sex ban.

In 2021, the International Olympic Committee implemented rules in an attempt to reduce contamination by the fast-spreading coronavirus. One of these rules banned intimacy between athletes in the village.

To prevent the athletes from having sex, they were provided with tiny and flimsy beds, which were made out of cardboard and what looked to be polystyrene foam.

If you’d like to view this content, please adjust yourCookie Settings.

To find out more about how we use cookies, please see ourCookie Guide.

Despite the ban being lifted for this year’s Olympics in Paris, the anti-sex beds remain in what seems like an attempt to continue the prevention of sexual extracurricular activities.

Paris organisers are said to be providing the athletes with around 300,000 condoms with the hopes the athletes will take them back home and spread awareness around safe sex as part of the Olympic tradition, which began in Seoul in 1998 to raise awareness for the HIV and AIDS epidemic.

Yesterday, Aussie water polo players Tilly Kearns and Gabi Palm delivered their verdict on anti-sex beds in a video uploaded to Tilly Kearns’ Instagram.

“Night one in the cardboard beds,” Kearns said.

To which Palm responded, “My back is about to fall off”.

Kearns captioned the post, “already had a message to undo the damage”.

In the video, Palm told Kearns, “It’s actually rock solid. You can kind of move it. Apparently there is a softer side,” she said while flipping the flimsy mattress.

“Oh, that was the soft side,” she said disappointedly.

The comments were flooded with shocked supporters questioning the intentions of the Paris Olympic organisers.

“The French did this deliberately to sabotage other nations teams 😂😂😂” one follower wrote.

“This is how France is getting its competitive advantage 🤪” another wrote.

Another added, “Is this the Olympic Games or the Hunger Games… seriously how do they expect elite athletes to achieve peak performance while sleeping on beds worse than you’d find in a backpackers.”

Tennis star Daria Saville and teammate Ellen Perez wore green and gold as they put their anti-sex bed to the ultimate test in a video uploaded to Daria Seville’s Instagram.

If you’d like to view this content, please adjust yourCookie Settings.

To find out more about how we use cookies, please see ourCookie Guide.

The pair alternated as they performed a series of high-intensity activities on the rock-solid bed to test its durability, including running on it, the iconic dance move ‘The Worm’, squat jumps, racquet smashing, and a whole lot of laughing.

The girls officially de-bunked the no-sex theory with their elaborate display of cardio, and followers couldn’t get enough.

“Stop the Olympics now; the highlight has been reached,” one wrote.

Another added, “This will be more entertaining than the Opening Ceremony 🤣🤣🤣.”
title: Intimacy ban lifted but anti-sex beds still haunt the 2024 Olympic athletes in Paris | PerthNow
description: Aussie water polo and tennis athletes have fans in stitches as they review their first night’s sleep in the Olympic village bed’s which were constructed for the Tokyo games in 2021 amid a sex-ban.
